records.summary: I18n TranslationBinding lifecycle errors are modeled after runtime rendering joins key and parameter bindings on the same target element.
records.rationale: Aurelia i18n does not treat each t/t.bind/t-params.bind attribute as an isolated binding lifetime. The renderer retrieves or creates one TranslationBinding per target and t-params.bind calls useParameter(...), so semantic-runtime diagnostics for AUR4000/AUR4001/AUR4002 must group rendered TranslationBinding products by target before publishing RuntimeBindingIssue products.
guidance: Inspect framework i18n TranslationBinding and translation renderers before changing this lane; the join point is TranslationBinding.useParameter(...).

Keep translation-key catalog products separate from translation-binding lifecycle diagnostics: catalog completion comes from configuration resources, while lifecycle errors come from rendered binding products.
Surface i18n lifecycle diagnostics through shared template/app diagnostic projection instead of adding i18n-specific API rows prematurely.

anchors.summary: Exact framework error-code links for AUR4000, AUR4001, and AUR4002.
Synthetic pressure fixture for i18n translation binding diagnostics.
Ground i18n error-code coverage before adding more i18n diagnostics.
